But the more important thing is lack of motive to work due to the across political parties freebies culture where if one can earn 40% of monthly salary by sitting at home in the form of freebies , then get free ration from the government and also other schemes for unemployment incentives , the incentive to work reduces drastically and we are seeing this slippery slope of lack of productivity only increase with all parties encouraging freebies !

items.title: Share of new and second-hand vehicle registrations. #Mauritius Our Understanding Mauritian new vehicle registrations simply refuse to budge, parked ne...
items.description_html:
Share of new and second-hand vehicle registrations. #Mauritius

Our Understanding

Mauritian new vehicle registrations simply refuse to budge, parked near a 70% share for over two years, spiking to 77% every January and easing to roughly 64% by June. The read for allocators is clean, the financing and import pipe feeding new sales looks healthy with no sign of cracking yet.

That hands the edge to new vehicle dealers and the lenders writing loans behind seven of every ten registrations, while sellers of used imports get only a brief midyear window. This split is not close at all. The structural money sits squarely on the new side, and it has comfortably stayed parked there.

So the lean is new vehicle retail and auto financing as the core exposure, with used imports worth watching purely as a seasonal trade. Nothing shifts that read while new share keeps orbiting 70%. Watch the June number closely, a clean break under 64% would flip the story toward real demand stress.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2056936672198352896

items.title: India curtailed 300 GWh of renewable generation in Q1 2026 due to transmission constraints ⚡ Battery storage is the fastest available fix and the eco...
items.description_html:
India curtailed 300 GWh of renewable generation in Q1 2026 due to transmission constraints

⚡ Battery storage is the fastest available fix and the economics already work:

📷 Solar + battery power: INR 7-8/kWh vs. 📷vs. 💹 Peak power procurement: INR 10/kWh

Our Understanding

Three hundred GWh of clean power went to waste early in 2026, not because India lacked generation but because the wires could not move it. That is a transmission problem. Batteries solve it, shifting solar into the evening peak at INR 7 to 8 per kWh against the INR 10 utilities are paying today.

Follow the money here. Solar tied to storage is where demand flows, and that pulls in battery cells, integrators and developers who can stitch the two together. Gas and diesel peakers are the clear losers, and every cheap stored unit chips away at what merchant sellers can extract in the peak window.

What to track is the grid scale storage and storage backed solar buildout, because the spread is now wide enough to drag procurement and tenders forward. The thesis cracks if battery cells get expensive again, if transmission charges jump, or if peak procurement slides back under the cost of stored solar.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2056936672198352896

items.title: The UK government is spinning its waiver on imports of Russian refined oil products as "temporary" (and briefing reporters it will be reviewed in 3 mo...
items.description_html:
The UK government is spinning its waiver on imports of Russian refined oil products as "temporary" (and briefing reporters it will be reviewed in 3 months).

But the legal text doesn't say that ⤵️. It says the waiver is of "indefinite duration." Of course, as any other waiver, it can be rescinded. But, if so, the legal text says the gov "will endevour" to provide the market with a 4-month notice. So, using the briefing guidance, that's 3 months for a review + 4 months notice = end of 2026.

Agree with the gov briefing in one aspect: cock-up.

items.title: Stylam Industries - Q4FY26 update🟩 The key update from the latest concall is that the new laminates plant is now close to commercial production The...
items.description_html:
Stylam Industries - Q4FY26 update🟩

The key update from the latest concall is that the new laminates plant is now close to commercial production

There was some delay mainly due to environment clearance requirements

Management indicated that the new plant will contribute 250-300 Cr REV in FY27

So, the direction remains positive, at peak utilisation it is expected to generate around 900-1,000 Cr REV

By FY28, it can potentially generate 600-700 Cr REV, assuming 60-70% utilisation

Demand commentary remains strong

Management mentioned that April was the best month in the company’s history, with sales crossing 100 Cr from the existing plant itself

In the Q4FY26 concall, management sounded more conservative & guided for around 20-25% growth over the medium term

However, considering April sales momentum, new plant contribution & operating leverage, there is room for positive surprise

Export market remains the key growth driver

Margin outlook remains strong

Management indicated that the new plant can operate at 22-24% margin profile

The important point is that most fixed costs, manpower cost & initial expenses of the new plant are already built into the current cost base

So, once the new plant starts contributing REV, operating leverage should flow through

A great story worth tracking, Markets already rewarding the company in anticipation of a bumper FY27.

Company gearing up for strong growth in FY27 as new capacity comes online

Management is targeting FY27 REV of 1500-1600 Cr

(Targeting ⏫40%+ Growth, TTM REV: 1112 Cr)

New laminates plant expected to contribute 300-400

items.title: RT Baijayant Jay Panda: Yet another proud moment for Odisha’s artisan legacy on the global stage. Prime Minister @narendramodi has gifted an intricat...
items.description_html:
RT Baijayant Jay Panda
Yet another proud moment for Odisha’s artisan legacy on the global stage. Prime Minister @narendramodi has gifted an intricate silver sailboat model from Cuttack to the King of Norway, along with a Palm Leaf Pattachitra to the Queen of Norway. Both these creations carry the distinct imprint of Odisha’s living traditions. The Tarakasi work, with its fine filigree detailing, reflects generations of mastery from Cuttack’s artisans, while Tala Pattachitra represents a uniquely Odia storytelling form etched with patience and precision on palm leaves. Meaningful gestures like these place Odisha’s heritage in a global context, quietly reinforcing the depth, continuity and confidence of India’s civilisational identity.

items.title: RT Kruti Shah: SpaceX IPO Key Takeaways Founder Control Dominates Governance: Elon Musk will control approximately 85.1% of voting power post-IPO thro...
items.description_html:
RT Kruti Shah
SpaceX IPO Key Takeaways

Founder Control Dominates Governance: Elon Musk will control approximately 85.1% of voting power post-IPO through a dual-class share structure, allowing him unilateral control over all shareholder matters including board elections and strategic direction.

Explosive Revenue Growth Masks Profitability Challenges: Revenue surged 33.2% year-over-year to $18.7 billion in 2025, but the company reported a $4.9 billion net loss driven by R&D spending that more than doubled to $8.6 billion.

Starlink Emerges as Primary Cash Engine: The connectivity segment has become the dominant profit driver with 9.2 million global subscribers by end of 2025 and segment EBITDA expanding to $7.2 billion, up from $3.8 billion in 2024.

Heavy Dependence on U.S. Government Contracts: SpaceX is the primary launch provider for the U.S. government, executing 11 of 12 National Security Space Launch missions in 2025, but faces concentration risk as these contracts worth approximately $13.7 billion can be modified or terminated at government discretion.

Record-Breaking IPO at Unprecedented Valuation: The anticipated $75 billion capital raise would be the largest in history, targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation that implies an extraordinary 109x Price-to-Sales multiple more than double the 1999 tech bubble peak.

Near-Monopoly in LEO Satellite Communications: SpaceX operates 10,281 LEO satellites representing 70.5% of all active low-Earth orbit satellites globally, maintaining a massive head start over Amazon's Project Kuiper which has deployed only 241 satellites.

Starship Commercialization Holds Transformative Potential: Successful deployment of the fully reusable Starship vehicle could drive launch costs below $200 per kilogram, potentially expanding the LEO market 100-fold and enabling deployment of up to 1 million satellites for orbital data centers.

items.title: RT IIT Bombay: On April 6, 2026, India achieved a historic breakthrough when the indigenously built Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam...
items.description_html:
RT IIT Bombay
On April 6, 2026, India achieved a historic breakthrough when the indigenously built Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam, Tamil Nadu, reached its first criticality. The milestone signifies India’s official transition to the second stage of its nuclear energy programme, making it only the second country in the world, after Russia, to operate a commercial-scale fast-breeder reactor.

Read more at: https://www.deccanherald.com/science/decoding-indias-kalpakkam-milestone-3980108

“Liquid Sodium poses challenges due to its chemical reactivity with air and water, and activation into radioactive sodium-24,” remarks Prof. Suneet Singh, professor at the Department of Energy Science and Engineering at IIT Bombay. “India’s FBR addresses these risks through a pool-type design of thermal stability and intermediate sodium loop to isolate radioactive coolant, and double-wall steam generators,” he adds.

items.title: India is considering incentives exceeding $1 billion to spur private-sector adoption of electric buses and trucks to cut fossil-fuel use amid a deepen...
items.description_html:
India is considering incentives exceeding $1 billion to spur private-sector adoption of electric buses and trucks to cut fossil-fuel use amid a deepening energy crisis.

Our Understanding

So India is thinking about putting more than $1 billion behind getting private fleets to buy electric buses and trucks, and what stands out is that the push is aimed at demand. You can see why. Electric is still only around 3% of new buses, so there is a long way to go before any of this shows up.

The way to think about who wins is simple enough. Domestic electric commercial vehicle makers, the battery suppliers and the charging builders all stand to benefit, and so do the fleet financiers. On the losing side you have the diesel engine makers and the fuel chain, which just keep slowly giving up ground.

If you are allocating, the safer way to play it is at the chain level, favouring electric commercial vehicles and charging over the diesel side. But keep one eye on the trigger. If the incentives stall or never really pay out, electric stays near 3% and the whole read quietly stops working.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2056936672198352896

items.title: Weight of WPI Components linked to crude oil & natural gas Approximately 1/3rd of the components are directly linked to crude/crude derivatives within...
items.description_html:
Weight of WPI Components linked to crude oil & natural gas

Approximately 1/3rd of the components are directly linked to crude/crude derivatives within the WPI basket

Our understading

About a third of the WPI basket is tied to crude oil and natural gas, so India's wholesale inflation is mostly an imported energy story, not a domestic demand one. When crude moves, it reaches fuels, petrochemicals and energy heavy metals within a quarter. The read fails only if WPI stops tracking crude.

Soft crude eases input costs across chemicals, plastics, paints, tyres and metals, lifting downstream margins and cooling factory gate prices. Producers of crude petroleum and natural gas feel the other side as realisations slip, so the broad manufacturing economy gains while energy extraction loses.

The crude to WPI link is the thing to watch. Energy heavy downstream manufacturing looks better placed when crude stays soft, while upstream oil and gas sits under realisation pressure. The falsifier is simple, since a sustained crude decline that fails to soften WPI would break the pass through thesis.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2056936672198352896

items.title: Can Blinkit grow more from here? Here's what the management says - "Between FY23 and FY26, Blinkit's NOV grew at 104% CAGR. Growth rates are now natur...
items.description_html:
Can Blinkit grow more from here? Here's what the management says -

"Between FY23 and FY26, Blinkit's NOV grew at 104% CAGR. Growth rates are now naturally moderating off a much larger base. Over the next three years, NOV growth CAGR should easily be north of 60%. That translates to the business growing to >4x its current scale in three years.

Quick commerce today is still concentrated in the top 15-20 cities and in a relatively narrow set of categories. The headroom for growth on geography, assortment, and frequency is substantial.

Top eight cities are approaching coverage maturity, with 80-90% of pin codes already serviceable. Incremental geographic growth comes from cities beyond the top eight, where average pin code coverage is lower than 30%"

Src – Q4 Earnings Letter; no reco strictly

items.title: China's economy is losing momentum: Fixed-asset investment in China fell -1.6% YoY in the first 4 months of 2026, slipping back into contraction terri...
items.description_html:
China's economy is losing momentum:

Fixed-asset investment in China fell -1.6% YoY in the first 4 months of 2026, slipping back into contraction territory.

The decline was driven by a -13.7% year-to-date drop in property development, building on an -11.2% decline in the first 3 months of 2026.

Furthermore, industrial production rose just +4.1% YoY in April, the weakest reading since mid-2023.

At the same time, retail sales grew just +0.2% YoY, the worst reading since China's economy reopened after the pandemic in December 2022.

This comes as car sales dropped -15% YoY, the steepest decline since mid-2022.

Meanwhile, gold, silver, and jewelry sales collapsed -21% YoY, reversing sharply from the buying frenzy seen earlier this year when surging precious metal prices drove speculative demand.

China's economic slowdown is broadening.

items.title: Record numbers by Nvidia Nvidia reported Q1 fiscal 2027 total revenue of $82 billion, up 85% year over year and 20% sequentially, marking the third co...
items.description_html:
Record numbers by Nvidia

Nvidia reported Q1 fiscal 2027 total revenue of $82 billion, up 85% year over year and 20% sequentially, marking the third consecutive quarter of year over year acceleration and 14th straight quarter of sequential growth.

Data center revenue reached $75 billion, up 92% year over year and 21% sequentially, with hyperscale revenue at $38 billion and a new segment called ASCI reporting $37 billion, growing 31% quarter over quarter.

Nvidia returned a record $20 billion to shareholders through share repurchases while also making strategic investments in upstream supply chain and downstream go-to-market ecosystem.

The company introduced a new reporting framework with two market platforms, Data Center and Edge Computing, to better reflect current and future growth drivers, with historical data for the past nine quarters posted on their website.

items.title: Food is set to become even more expensive: World fertilizer prices have surged +44% since the start of the Iran War, to the highest since 2022. This c...
items.description_html:
Food is set to become even more expensive:

World fertilizer prices have surged +44% since the start of the Iran War, to the highest since 2022.

This comes as ~33% of globally traded fertilizers pass through the Strait of Hormuz, which remains effectively closed.

This includes 23% of global ammonia, 34% of urea, the world's most widely used nitrogen fertilizer, and nearly 20% of global phosphate supply.

Furthermore, the Bloomberg Agriculture Subindex has increased ~9% since the Iran war.

The index tracks the futures prices of key agricultural commodities, including wheat, corn, soybeans, sugar, coffee, and cotton.

In the past, world fertilizer prices have acted as a leading indicator for agricultural output prices, as rising production costs eventually force farmers to reduce supply, pushing crop prices higher.

A new wave of global food inflation is imminent.

Centre Rolls Out New Land Monetisation Rules To Unlock Massive Government Land Banks

The Centre has launched a major new framework under NMP 2.0 to unlock and monetise surplus government-owned land across India. The move replaces decades-old fragmented rules with a single system for identifying, valuing, transferring and commercially using public land assets held by ministries, Railways, defence establishments and state-run companies.

items.description_html:
BREAKING: Crypto ETFs posted -$1.1 billion in outflows last week, the first withdrawal in 7 weeks.

This also marks the largest weekly outflow since the beginning of February and 3rd-largest of 2026.

Bitcoin led the withdrawals at -$982 million, followed by Ethereum at -$249 million, the largest since the end of January.

However, Bitcoin has still attracted +$3.9 billion in total inflows year-to-date.

Furthermore, crypto fund total assets under management fell -$2.0 billion, to $157 billion.

Meanwhile, altcoins saw inflows last week, with XRP at +$68 million and Solana at +$55 million.

Crypto flows are becoming increasingly volatile.

RT Gurmeet Chadha
RBI announces $5 billion Dollar/ INR Swap on 26th May.. got some DMs. Will try and simplify

-basically commercial banks like SBI , hdfc etc will sell dollars to RBI and take Rupees in return
- after 3 years banks buy back the same amount of dollars & They have to return the rupees . This is obviously done at an agreed swap premium.

Why it is done - it infuses rupee liquidity ( banks get rupee)and also helps stabilise the currency as RBI gets dollars.

The distributed asset value of real-world tokenized assets is now up to a record $33.8 billion.

This represents a +1,600% increase over the last 2 years and adoption is only accelerating.

Growth in these assets gained momentum after onchain platforms like Jupiter began listing tokenized assets through partnerships with Securitize, xStocks, and Ondo finance, prompting +34% week-over-week volume growth.

Meanwhile, Bloomberg reported this week that the SEC is leaning toward allowing the trading of tokenized assets in a "surprise move."

This would mark one of the US' biggest shifts into crypto infrastructure yet.

Tokenization is taking over.

items.title: RBI announces USD 5 bn Buy/Sell Swap to inject liquidity RBI announced a 3-year USD/Rupee Buy/Sell swap auction of USD 5 billion to inject durable rup...
items.description_html:
RBI announces USD 5 bn Buy/Sell Swap to inject liquidity

RBI announced a 3-year USD/Rupee Buy/Sell swap auction of USD 5 billion to inject durable rupee liquidity into the banking system.

Dates 👉 May 26, 2026, with settlement on May 29, 2026. Under the transaction, RBI will inject durable rupee liquidity upfront.

Why this move is done?
👍 Aimed at maintaining comfortable system liquidity conditions, supporting short-term rates, while also augmenting FX reserves.

Nirmal Bang view 👉With the recent rise in crude oil prices bringing inflation concerns back, RBI is likely to rely more on liquidity infusion measures while keeping policy rates on hold. Expect the RBI to look through any supply-shock-driven inflation unless there is evidence of inflation expectations becoming entrenched.

items.title: Raamdeo Agrawal Sir on the inception of Motilal Oswal company - "For starting my business, I contacted Motilal Oswal ji . Actually Motilal ji was juni...
items.description_html:
Raamdeo Agrawal Sir on the inception of Motilal Oswal company -

"For starting my business, I contacted Motilal Oswal ji . Actually Motilal ji was junior to me by three years. I had never met him while we were in the same hostel, but I had seen him there. Around 1986,I was staying in Lokhandwala Complex, in Mumbai, with my brother, where Motilal ji was also staying.

I had a Hero Honda motorcycle. Since transportation was a problem in that area, I used to give him a lift to his work place. Over a period of time, I realised that he was also interested in the stock markets. I suggested to him that we should start something of our own.

I told him that I have no capital, no clients, but I was very confident about my knowledge of the markets. Motilal had two brothers - one in Chennai and another in Ahmedabad. They used to buy stocks in those exchanges and sell them in Mumbai. They were looking for a new broker in Mumbai for these transactions. Their earlier broker had once cheated them. I told Motilal Ji that we can partner with them. We would find a broker and become sub brokers and that would be a good start for our business.

That’s how the partnership started in 1987. We worked on a very tight budget for the first four years. We didn’t spend much on the business start-up costs. We soon started making around Rs. 1000 per day, and within four years, we were able to save about Rs. 15 lakh. One important thing was our principle of keeping the books clean and paying full income tax. I did not want any trouble with the Income Tax authorities. So we always kept clean books.

One can make a mistake, but there should be no doubt on integrity. We could not still afford the stock exchange membership as it required a capital of Rs. 25 lakh. So we borrowed Rs. 10-12 lakh and bought the BSE card. Then the Harshad Mehta bull-run happened. From that initial Rs. 10-15 lakh, we made around Rs. 30 crore in the next 18-24 months! In fact, I think that bull-run happened for us only, just like Y2K happened for India only"

From the Book Masterclass with Super Investors

Agriculture is both a victim of climate change and a contributor to it. In India, the sector accounts for roughly 16 per cent of the total greenhouse gas emissions, much of it stemming from the input-intensive rice-wheat systems promoted aggressively since the Green Revolution. Legumes have the power to help India escape this vicious cycle, while slashing the country's dependence on imported fertilisers and pulses.

items.title: PEB / Prefab Sector - Q4FY26 Key Insights Now let’s discuss each company in detail, one by one - 1️⃣ Interarch Building 2️⃣ EPACK Prefab 3️⃣ M&...
items.description_html:
PEB / Prefab Sector - Q4FY26 Key Insights

Now let’s discuss each company in detail, one by one -

1️⃣ Interarch Building
2️⃣ EPACK Prefab
3️⃣ M&B Engineering

Interarch Building : FY26 highlights
REV - 1,898 Cr 🔼31%
EBITDA - 176 Cr 🔼29%
PAT - 135 Cr 🔼25%

Management Commentary & Future Outlook
- Targeting REV : 2,200 Cr/2,500 Cr by FY27/FY28
- Order book stood at 1,703 Cr as of April'26
- 127 Cr spent in Capex, expanding capacities in Andhra Pradesh, Kiccha & Gujarat
- Andhra Pradesh & Gujarat plants are expected to be commissioned by Q2FY27
- Andhra Pradesh expansion is focused on heavy steel structures
- Gujarat PEB plant is located in a major industrial cluster & targets sectors like EVs and semiconductors
- Company secured export certifications for Canada and USA
- Export orders worth over 40 Cr were received in FY26
- MoU signed with ER Steel for structural steel & PEB business across Canada and North America
- Total REV potential : 3500 Cr after all capacities come live

EPACK Prefab Technologies : FY26 highlights
REV - 1,525 Cr 🔼35%
EBITDA - 160 Cr 🔼36%
PAT - 93 Cr 🔼56%

Management Commentary & Future Outlook
- FY27 REV guidance to surpass 1,900 Cr
- Management is guiding for 30% growth in the prefab division
- EBITDA margin guidance remains 10.5%
- Order book stood at 1,113 Cr as of March’26
- Prefab business grew around 45% YoY in FY26
- Capacity utilisation improved through the year & reached 83% in Q4FY26
- Operating cash flow stood at 136 Cr, around 85% of EBITDA, Net cash at 201 Cr
- Management believes 30% growth is achievable in Prefab division based on order book, pipeline & capacity additions

M&B Engineering : FY26 highlights
REV - 1,260 Cr 🔼27%
PAT - 93 Cr 🔼20%

Management Commentary & Future Outlook
- FY27 growth guidance stands at 23-25%
- Management has not given specific margin guidance due to uncertainty in steel prices, freight, FX & geopolitical conditions
- Unexecuted order book stood at 1,083 Cr, growing 35% YoY
- FY26 order inflow stood at 1,539 Cr 🔼28%
- FY26 capex was 33 Cr, while FY27 capex is expected around 100 Cr
- Phenix division FY26 REV stood at 985 Cr 🔼29%
- Proflex division FY26 REV stood at 275 Cr 🔼23%
- Company is adding around 20,000 MT capacity at Sanand, expected to be commissioned in Q2FY27
- Export REV target is around 275-300 Cr in FY27 vs 165 Cr in FY26
- Export EBITDA margins are expected at 16-17% vs domestic margins of around 10-11%
- Margin pressure in Q4 was due to FX loss, input cost inflation & freight costs

Sector continue to witness tailwinds from industrial capex, warehousing, logistics, renewable energy, data centres, semiconductors & large manufacturing projects

There is a structural shift visible from conventional construction to faster, factory made & execution efficient solutions

If you want us to cover other sectors in a similar way, do mention below. Also, let us know which sector should be covered first.

items.title: PEB sector was one of the most chased themes recently The reason was simple Sector had strong tailwinds, good order books, capacity expansion visibili...
items.description_html:
PEB sector was one of the most chased themes recently

The reason was simple

Sector had strong tailwinds, good order books, capacity expansion visibility & demand visibility from industrial capex, warehousing, logistics, data centres, renewables, EVs & manufacturing

Because of this, valuations expanded very sharply
Most were trading sub 20x in the space but once it caught market attention it even moved towards 35x earnings

At that stage, the story was good, but the valuation comfort was missing

When a sector becomes crowded, more often than not, it disappoints

Most of these companies are now trading below 20x trailing earnings & around 15-16x on fwd earnings

This changes the risk reward meaningfully.

So the real question is -
Is the PEB story over? Absolutely not.

PEB is clearly gaining share from conventional construction because it saves time, improves execution speed & gives better scalability for industrial customers. So the sector opportunity remains intact.

But is the hype over? Absolutely yes.

Recent Correction has removed a large part of the excess valuation froth

Earlier, investors were paying a premium for the same story.

Now, the same theme is available at much more reasonable valuations

When everyone starts chasing the same theme, valuations can move far ahead of earnings

At that point, even a small miss in margins, execution or guidance can lead to sharp correction

The takeaway is simple. Do not chase hype. It never ends well.

This is the biggest lesson from the PEB space.

items.title: The case for betting on Integrated Solar Players Chinese players like Longi, jinko, Tina, JA Solar dominate because of vertical integration Waaree and...
items.description_html:
The case for betting on Integrated Solar Players

Chinese players like Longi, jinko, Tina, JA Solar dominate because of vertical integration

Waaree and few others are trying to replicate that in India

The industry is not protected by Patents but from Vertical integration, Balance Sheet Strength/Capital, Execution, tech know how and Supply Chain control

Without upstream integration, a company can be dependent on Chinese wafers/cells/polysilicon

Solar value chain: Quartz → Polysilicon → Ingot → Wafer → Cell → Module with each layer having its own margin pool.

If a player integrates upward and backward, instead of earning only module economics, it can capture multiple profit pools.

This is exactly what Asian manufacturing giants did.

Src –Waaree unbound 2.0 PPT , other analysis

items.title: India’s Internet economy has a long runway across multiple verticals 1. E-commerce remains one of the largest opportunities, with current GMV around ...
items.description_html:
India’s Internet economy has a long runway across multiple verticals

1. E-commerce remains one of the largest opportunities, with current GMV around $70 Bn & expected to reach $174-214 Bn by FY30.

2. B2B e-commerce looks like the biggest structural white space. Current TAM is $45 Bn with less than 2% digital penetration, but the market opportunity can scale towards $2.5 Tn by FY30.

3. Food delivery is already more consolidated, with Zomato + Swiggy holding around 85% share. Growth is still healthy at 17-23% CAGR, but the market is more mature compared now.

4. Quick commerce is the fastest growing theme. The market has grown 100% YoY from $6.2 Bn in FY25 to nearly $11-12 Bn in FY26E.

Rest mentioned⏬

The largest opportunities are in B2B e-commerce, e-commerce, quick commerce, food delivery & mobility.

src : ICICI Sec

items.title: India Power Sector - key insights India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26 FY26 total capacity addition at 58 GW Renewable generation ...
items.description_html:
India Power Sector - key insights

India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26

FY26 total capacity addition at 58 GW

Renewable generation grew 20%

Installed capacity mix
Thermal capacity at 249 GW
Solar capacity at 150 GW
Wind capacity at 56 GW
Hydro capacity at 51 GW
Nuclear capacity at 9 GW

Solar capacity has increased from 22 GW in FY18 to 150 GW in FY26

That is almost 7x growth in 8 years
FY30E at 288 GW

Cell manufacturing capacity is expected to rise from 25 GW in FY25 to 115 GW by FY30E

Ingot-wafer capacity is expected to rise from 2 GW in FY25 to 80 GW by FY30E

Polysilicon capacity is also expected to start scaling from FY27 onwards

Earlier, the opportunity was mainly in module assembly

Now the opportunity is expanding into cells, wafers, ingots & polysilicon

Key numbers: • EBITDA grew 30%+ to ~₹90 Cr, Revenue grew by 34%
• PAT rose 86% to ~₹100 Cr
• Distribution expanded in North India
• Exports entered new geographies

The interesting part is the category.

Biscuits and affordable snacks are everyday consumption products. That makes demand relatively more resilient, even when costs and logistics become uncertain.

For Nurture Well, the next phase depends on execution — scaling capacity, managing supply chain pressure, and converting export opportunities into sustainable growth.

items.title: Bajaj Finance on AI deployment and transformation in Q4FY26 Concall - "We expect FY27 to be probably the busiest year from a FinAI transformation in t...
items.description_html:
Bajaj Finance on AI deployment and transformation in Q4FY26 Concall -

"We expect FY27 to be probably the busiest year from a FinAI transformation in terms of outcomes, inputs, and outputs as a firm, and a significant amount of time, effort, and energy of the management team over the last 15-18 months has gone into it. And should start to reflect in terms of the way consumers and employees experience us as a company in FY27

Just to give you some texture on the transformation work, the biggest part of AI is about whether you have the talent or not. We have 203 people in the firm who are dedicated to the AI unit. We are expanding that to 363-odd resources by June'27. So we continue to expand the AI resourcing to ensure it can meet our aspirations and ambitions of becoming a FinAI firm.

Step number 1 in FinAI transformation clearly is about Data for AI, 52 million voice-to-data conversion happened, around 2.3 million text-to-data conversion happened, and led to significantly better insights on engagements or conversations with customers. AI-generated digital banners and videos are enabling faster and cost-efficient marketing and discovery infrastructure.

~100% of our videos are now AI-generated. On customer engagement, 27 AI, voice, and text BOTs are now live, and all customer engagement is going into BOT interface by June 26. By June 26, any communication across any channel by us as a firm, whether for sales, service, or DMS, would have a text AI BOT in it. So across 32 different lines of business that we run, plus a whole host of services.

And we send, as I've said in the past, 260-odd million pieces of communication on a monthly basis. All of them will have a bot embedded in it. On customer onboarding, AI-based data extraction, auto-fill application, product-level scans, label scans, and auto QC are increasingly reducing manual effort, improving controllership, and straight-through processing.

The most important dimension - we'll start to deploy agentic platforms from an architecture and road map standpoint. In FY27, we expect to deploy 600-plus autonomous agents across operations, DMS, HR, technology, and risk."

Src - Q4FY26 Concall; no reco

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CITI On Axis Bank Buy, TP Rs 1620 Management meet takeaways [1] West Asia crisis – No visible deterioration and remains comfortable o...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CITI On Axis Bank
Buy, TP Rs 1620
Management meet takeaways
[1] West Asia crisis – No visible deterioration and remains comfortable on growth and asset quality; likely carries sufficient buffer to absorb most adverse scenario.
[2] Disciplined growth strategy: To outperform system average growth; critically growth will not come at the cost of risk discipline.
[3] Committed to through-the-cycle NIM of 3.8% given structural levers; we expect some near-term NIM pressure from the mix shift.
[4] ECL transition impact on networth to be marginal; structurally marginal increase in credit cost (sector-wide dynamic).
[5] Strategic deposit franchise deepening underway; comfortable operating at ~92% LDR +/- 2-3%.
[6] Likely to deliver positive operating leverage (on C/I basis) in FY27 and cost to assets will be optimized over medium term

CITI on ICICI BK
Buy, TP Rs 1720
Management meet Key takeaways
[1] Broad-based growth momentum led by step-up in retail (mortgages, PL, GL); corporate growth supported by a structural shift in funding from bond markets to banks; continued momentum in business banking.
[2] NIMs range-bound with mix optimization the lever, while CoD may remain elevated.
[3] West Asia crisis shows no visible portfolio stress; actively monitoring borrowers exposed to global trade and supply chain disruptions; tighter credit filters on new borrower onboarding in vulnerable sectors.
[4] Fee income trajectory likely to improve toward double-digit growth; opex discipline maintained with growth expected to be below revenue/asset growth.
[5] ECL transition - given existing buffers, no networth impact anticipated; credit cost to marginally rise on a flow basis.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: Recently spoke to a veteran in Transmission industry. 7 Key takeaways 1. HVDC is a huge bottleneck and robust order pipeline is likely to persist here...
items.description_html:
Recently spoke to a veteran in Transmission industry.

7 Key takeaways

1. HVDC is a huge bottleneck and robust order pipeline is likely to persist here till FY30. Only 3 cos in the world have the technology and few Indian HVDC component cos are supporting their scale up.

2. Lot of the Transformer cos in IDT segment are commoditised, and will feel the heat as the commodity price hike is capped at 10% in a lot of government contracts.

3. Transformer oil prices have gone from 70-75 rs to 170 rs+ Per litre, which is impacting the GP margins directly.

4. There is a 18month+ wait period for the supply of insulators. Prices are up 100%+ and companies are working on advance payment.

5. Winding Wires/Copper wires as a segment doesn't have a huge moat. Some of these cos can see working capital spiking big time and lot of profits are just inventory gains.

6. Many transformer cos are backwards integrating and doing things like CRGO coils in house.

7. Power transformers: 400 KVA and 765 KVA transformers are the toughest category to enter in. Export orders are abundant in this category if someone cracks it.

In conclusion, Bottlenecks in India ecosystems exists in

HVDC & components
Insulators
Higher voltage products
Few niche tech cos in Gas gensets & turbines

Since 2014 we continue to serve every sector and every section of our society through our policies and initiatives.
Farmers, the poor, women, youth, MSMEs are at the core of our governance structure.

items.title: RT Rihard Jarc: A MUST-read interview with a Siemens employee explaining just how high demand is for energy equipment right now because of AI: 1. The ...
items.description_html:
RT Rihard Jarc
A MUST-read interview with a Siemens employee explaining just how high demand is for energy equipment right now because of AI:

1. The whole situation is shocking even for people who have been in the business for 40 years. They are getting orders that are double the size of what their entire factory can produce in a year.

2. Demand is so high in the last 5-8 months that they don't need to convince or send any analysis (such as CO2 emissions, etc.) to clients because they just want the equipment, because there's so much backlog that they just want to catch the order.

3. Decisions are being made very quickly by clients; the backlog for some of the energy equipment companies is 5-6 years. For transformers, the situation is even more difficult.

4. He mentions that right now, data center builders do not care about sustainability; they just want power at any expense, reliable power. They say they will think about sustainability later.

5. The orders have gone from previous 20-30 MW orders to now 200-500 MW units. Customers have previously wanted to get equipment from different OEMs, but now they prefer an integrated standardized solution.

6. An interesting dynamic is that even though the data center requires 100 MW, the builders are buying N+1 units of gas turbines (so more than just for 100 MW) as backups, as well as having more energy capacity, as they believe they will continue to grow that data center.

7. He does believe there is some double booking going on on transformers and switchgears because of extra-long lead times.

8. Everyone is trying to reduce PUE, and water use effectiveness, but even after improving, they just use the same power to run more compute.

9. The problem is also liquid cooling, as it is expensive, and water availability in many regions is a problem.

10. Margins on equipment in the sector have gone from 4-6%, where they were 2-3 years ago, to 20-23% and in some cases even 40%. The data center builders know the margins are high, but they are fine with it because they just want to get it.

found on @AlphaSenseInc

items.title: RT ANI: #WATCH | Mumbai: Chief Economist, CareEdge Ratings, Rajani Sinha says, “India's forex reserves are falling mainly because of weak capital flo...
items.description_html:
RT ANI
#WATCH | Mumbai: Chief Economist, CareEdge Ratings, Rajani Sinha says, “India's forex reserves are falling mainly because of weak capital flows into the country. Because of this, we are seeing a sharp weakening of the currency, and the RBI has been intervening in the forex market to control the depreciation of the rupee. That is why, since the start of the West Asia crisis, we have seen our forex reserves deplete by around 27 to 28 billion dollars. When I say capital flows, not only are we seeing high FII outflows since the West Asia crisis started—so for March and April, cumulatively we have seen FII outflows of around $20 billion—but if we look at the whole of the last fiscal year, we also saw FII outflows of around $16 billion or so…”

items.title: RT CMO Maharashtra: 🤝CM Devendra Fadnavis presided over the signing and exchange of MoUs between the Government of Maharashtra (Energy Department) ...
items.description_html:
RT CMO Maharashtra
🤝CM Devendra Fadnavis presided over the signing and exchange of MoUs between the Government of Maharashtra (Energy Department) and various companies in Mantralaya, Mumbai, today.

Details of the MOUs:

🔸Adani Power Limited and its Coastal-Maha Atomic Energy Limited

For setting up a nuclear power plant at Barsu, Dist. Ratnagiri.
✅Proposed Capacity: 6000 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹1,50,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 4500 + 7500 = 12,000

🔸Reliance Industries Ltd.

For setting up Bharat Small Reactors/Small Modular Reactor (SMR)/Nuclear Power Reactors at Purnagadh, Dist. Ratnagiri.
✅Proposed Capacity: 2 X 220 MW to 6 X 1200 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹8000 to ₹2,00,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 3000 + 1,00,000 = 1,03,000

🔸NTPC (National Thermal Power Corporation) Ltd.

For setting up a nuclear power plant at Devgad, Dist. Sindhudurg.
✅Proposed Capacity: 6 Units/ 4000 MW - 7200 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹1,00,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 5000

Minister Atul Save, Business Head, Nuclear Energy at Adani Group, Vivek Sharma, President Nuclear Energy Business Reliance Industries Ltd., Ranjay Sharan, CEO, NTPC, Arnada Prasad Samal and other senior officials were present.

🤝 मुख्यमंत्री देवेंद्र फडणवीस यांच्या प्रमुख उपस्थितीत आज मंत्रालय, मुंबई येथे महाराष्ट्र शासन (ऊर्जा विभाग) आणि विविध कंपन्यांमध्ये सामंजस्य करार करण्यात आले.

सामंजस्य करारांचे तपशील

🔸अदानी पॉवर लिमिटेड व त्यांची कोस्टल-महा ॲटोमिक एनर्जी लिमिटेड

बारसू, जि. रत्नागिरी येथे अणुऊर्जा प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता - 6000 मेगावॅट
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक - ₹1,50,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 4500 + 7500 = 12,000

🔸 रिलायन्स इंडस्ट्रीज लिमिटेड

पूर्णगड जि. रत्नागिरी येथे 'भारत स्मॉल रिॲक्टर्स' / स्मॉल मॉड्युलर रिॲक्टर (SMR)/ अणुऊर्जा रिॲक्टर प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता : 2 X 220 MW ते 6 X 1200 MW
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक : ₹8000 ते ₹2,00,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 3000 + 1,00,000 = 1,03,000

🔸 नॅशनल थर्मल पॉवर कॉर्पोरेशन लिमिटेड (NTPC)

सिंधुदुर्ग जिल्ह्यात देवगड येथे अणुऊर्जा प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता : एकूण 6 युनिट्सद्वारे 4000 ते 7200 मेगावॅट
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक : ₹1,00,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 5000

यावेळी मंत्री अतुल सावे, अदानी समूहाच्या न्यूक्लिअर एनर्जीचे बिझनेस हेड विवेक शर्मा, रिलायन्स इंडस्ट्रीज लिमिटेडच्या न्यूक्लिअर एनर्जी बिझनेसचे अध्यक्ष, रंजय शरण, एनटीपीसीचे मुख्य कार्यकारी अधिकारी अर्णदा प्रसाद सामल आणि वरिष्ठ अधिकारी उपस्थित होते.

items.title: BSE is not a part of NIFTY100 hence it's not possible to include in NIFTY50 for current review ? Rationale as per Quant Analyst • BSE Ltd. not includ...
items.description_html:
BSE is not a part of NIFTY100 hence it's not possible to include in NIFTY50 for current review ?

Rationale as per Quant Analyst

• BSE Ltd. not included in Nifty 50 as the stock was not forming part/ going to form part of Nifty 100 index
• Constituents of Nifty 100 index that are available for trading in NSE's Futures & Options segment are eligible for inclusion in the Nifty 50 index. The latest composition of Nifty 100 including most recent changes whether announced or yet to be announced shall be considered eligible subject to availability of trading in NSE's Futures & Options segment in such stocks
• Securities shall be considered eligible for inclusion in the Nifty 100, subject to a maximum of 5 replacements during a review, if ranked among the top 90 based on average full market capitalisation based on average data for 6-month period as of the cut-off date
• BSE Ltd. not included in Nifty 100 as the stock was not forming part of top 90 as per full mcap based on average data for 6-month period ending 31st january 2026

• The review of Nifty 50 is undertaken semi-annually based on data for six months ending January and July. The replacement of stocks in Nifty 50 (if any) is implemented from the last trading day of March and September.

Adhoc review conducted only for events that involve exclusion of existing constituent of index. It's to be seen of BSE makes it to NIFTY 50 !?


Soumeet Sarkar: BSE and Wipro In Focus

BSE to get included in Nifty 50

Inclusion could lead to inflows of $657 million

Wipro to get excluded from Nifty 50

Exclusion could lead to outflows of $225 millon

items.title: RT Pulse Of Profit: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd corrected nearly 40% recently amid concerns around weak operating cash flow and rising receivables. @T...
items.description_html:
RT Pulse Of Profit
Jain Resource Recycling Ltd corrected nearly 40% recently amid concerns around weak operating cash flow and rising receivables.

@Tijori1

But management says the issue is largely accounting-related, not operational 👇

Key clarification from the concall:

• Reported cash flow was impacted due to NFRA accounting requirements
• Receivables discounted with banks now need to be shown separately instead of netting off
• Management claims actual cash flows remain healthy
Other important points:
• Copper prices & volumes up 26% in recent months
• No fresh bank borrowing in the last 4 months
• Working capital funded through internal accruals
• Inventory levels already reduced from Q3 to Q4
• Targeting reduction in working capital cycle from 66 days to below 60 days
• Receivable days currently managed at 15–18 days
Management expects working capital to turn positive from Q2 onwards.

Important to watch now:
👉 Whether upcoming quarters validate management commentary through improving cash generation and lower working capital intensity.

items.title: One of the most important measures of a country's nutritional and public health status is whether its children are adequately nourished. The most clos...
items.description_html:
One of the most important measures of a country's nutritional and public health status is whether its children are adequately nourished. The most closely tracked indicator on child nutrition outcomes is stunting.

A child is considered stunted if their height is more than two standard deviations lower than the median for children of the same age and gender, using World Health Organization growth standards.

In 1993, more than one in two children under the age of five in India was stunted. By 2022, this rate dropped to one in three, indicating a decline in stunting over time. Even then, the levels of child stunting in India remain high and are comparable to those seen in Sub-Saharan African nations with much lower per capita incomes.

Stunting affects 30% of urban children, while the rate is higher in rural areas at 37%. Across both sectors, children from the richest households are about half as likely to be stunted compared to those from the poorest households.

These persistently high rates of stunting in India despite economic growth have led to some debate around the WHO's international growth standards. However, research suggests that the high rates of stunting in India reflect a combination of multiple underlying factors, rather than just issues with the standards themselves.

How does stunting affect children from different regions across India? Read @nileena_suresh's analysis to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/child-stunting/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Health #PublicHealth #Child #Stunting #India #DataForIndia

RT Normal Guy
Koreans are surrendering life insurance policies to buy SK hynix, Insurance surrenders at the top 3 life insurers jumped 16% last quarter.

Savings bank deposits fell below 100 trillion won for the first time in 4 years. Commercial bank time deposits dropped 12 trillion won since February.

The entire financial system is being rerouted into 2 semiconductor stocks. When new money into a rally comes not from income but from liquidating safety nets, that is how the end of every cycle looks man, this is crazy .....

Investors over 50 now hold 62% of margin loans at Korea top 10 brokerages. Among those in their 60s, margin debt doubled from 3.9 to 8 trillion won in one year.

These are people who spent decades in fixed deposits and real estate, now entering a semiconductor rally on borrowed money at record highs.

When the KOSPI dropped 19% in March, leveraged investors in their 60s lost 20% on average. The rally recovered.

RT Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent
.@USTreasury is issuing a temporary 30-day general license to provide the most vulnerable nations with the ability to temporarily access Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

This extension will provide additional flexibility, and we will work with these nations to provide specific licenses as needed. This general license will help stabilize the physical crude market and ensure oil reaches the most energy-vulnerable countries.

It will also help reroute existing supply to countries most in need by reducing China’s ability to stockpile discounted oil.

items.title: Raamdeo Agrawal Sir on his first 100 bagger - "My first 100-bagger was Vysya Bank in 1991. I had bought 2500 shares. It was bought on the advice of a ...
items.description_html:
Raamdeo Agrawal Sir on his first 100 bagger -

"My first 100-bagger was Vysya Bank in 1991. I had bought 2500 shares. It was bought on the advice of a professional client in Chennai. The stock went from Rs.20 to Rs. 2000 within two years. Then it started coming down. It came down to Rs.500

We had no idea why it went up or came down. We had barely seen the balance sheet. Then I was told to sell the stock at Rs. 500 before it goes back to Rs. 20!

Somehow, the delivery parcel was lost, and it took another six months to get duplicate shares. We had to pay an additional Rs. 100 per share for squaring off. So my actual cost became Rs. 120. But by then, the share had again gone to Rs.2500. I then sold it.

Sometimes, just by sitting, one makes money by luck. The key learning from this was that you can make 100 times your money in the stock market. I always found the stock market to be very magical. This was the first time I experienced making 100x returns on my money on a stock."

From the Book Masterclass with Super Investors; no reco

items.description_html:
RT Gaurav Kapadia
I am thrilled to announce the launch of http://Totei.com.

Totei is a magazine devoted to craft and craftsmanship in all its forms. The name Totei comes from the ancient Japanese word for apprentice.

I have always been inspired by those with a deep devotion to their craft—across every discipline. Making something truly remarkable requires extraordinary dedication, and the creative process behind it is rarely seen.

That curiosity is why I started Totei. My hope is that everyone who reads it feels the same sense of inspiration I do when getting inside the minds of exceptional makers.

items.title: "Over the past year, the global solar industry has entered a new phase. Markets are becoming more selective, supply chains are being reconfigured, and...
items.description_html:
"Over the past year, the global solar industry has entered a new phase. Markets are becoming more selective, supply chains are being reconfigured, and the domestic manufacturing capability is becoming increasingly important across regions.

In this environment, scale alone is no longer sufficient. Integration, technology alignment, and execution, reliability are becoming the defining factors for long-term competitiveness.

ALMM 1 created the foundation for approved module manufacturing. The next phase is more important for integrated manufacturers.

ALMM List 2 for cells is expected to deepen domestic cell sourcing, while ALMM List 3 for wafers and ingots will gradually move the framework further upstream from 2028.

We believe the long-term opportunity will not belong only to companies with module manufacturing; it will belong to companies that can participate deeper in the value chain, maintain quality, improve traceability, and reduce dependence on external supply chains

Schemes such as PM Surya Ghar, PM KUSUM, and other domestic content-linked programs are also creating a more stable demand environment for Indian manufacturers."

- Emmvee Photovoltaic Q4 Concall, no reco

Tata Electronics said 60% of demand is in these sizes

28nm and above = 65–75% of unit demand
Advanced nodes (<10nm) dominate revenue, not volume

Think of semiconductors like vehicles.

3nm = Formula 1 car - insanely fast, expensive, niche
28nm = Toyota Innova - practical, reliable, huge demand
130nm = tractors/trucks - not glamorous, but the economy runs on them

items.title: Reading is only half the craft. The other half is pattern recognition: noticing when an idea from years ago suddenly explains a situation unfolding in...
items.description_html:
Reading is only half the craft. The other half is pattern recognition: noticing when an idea from years ago suddenly explains a situation unfolding in real time.

One line from Ben Horowitz stayed with me: “If you are going to eat shit, don’t nibble.” Crude, but deeply practical. The lesson isn’t about startups alone; it’s about confronting reality fully instead of managing optics incrementally.

The best readers don’t just collect quotes. They build heuristics, then wait for life – markets, businesses, leadership, and relationships – to reveal where those models apply.

A good book becomes valuable twice: once when you read it, and again when reality makes you understand it.

This particular stock has seen a noticeable re-rating in recent days. Even today it's positively reacting to Q4 FY26 earnings.

https://antifragilethinking.substack.com/p/five-earnings-calls-zero-red-flags

items.title: RT The Kobeissi Letter: It simply won't stop. It is Sunday night and the US 10Y Note Yield just casually hit 4.63%, the highest since February 2025. W...
items.description_html:
RT The Kobeissi Letter
It simply won't stop.

It is Sunday night and the US 10Y Note Yield just casually hit 4.63%, the highest since February 2025.

We are now ~4 basis points ABOVE the high that prompted President Trump's "90-day tariff pause" in April 2025.

This puts the 10Y Note Yield up +70 basis points since the Iran War, with US mortgage rates now nearing 7.00%+.

And, in a sudden turn of events, the odds of rate cuts have collapsed to 2% this year and US inflation is nearing 4%+.

The US bond market is collapsing in real-time.

The largest Tunnel Boring Machine (TBM) cutter head ever used in railway construction in India is now being deployed for the Mumbai–Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail Corridor 🔥

With a massive 13.6-meter diameter, this first-of-its-kind cutter head has been lowered into the Vikhroli shaft as part of the assembly of TBM S1393. A similar TBM, S1392, is also being assembled at the Ghansoli–Sawli shaft.

A total 21 km underground section between BKC and Shilphata on the MAHSR will be excavated using these advanced tunnel boring machines. 🚆🔥

items.title: RT Nitin Pai: Re Distrust of civic institutions compounds the pre-existing generalized distrust in our hyper-diverse society, leaving India with a sev...
items.description_html:
RT Nitin Pai
Re Distrust of civic institutions compounds the pre-existing generalized distrust in our hyper-diverse society, leaving India with a severe social capital deficit.

We buy gold to cover this deficit.

Gold is imported and paid for in foreign currency, and hence shows up on the deficit side of the current account.

As CapitalMind CEO @deepakshenoy showed, if gold imports are excluded, India’s current account would have been in surplus for 11 of the past 12 quarters.

In other words, our social capital deficit is exacerbating macroeconomic problems during an approaching crisis.

items.title: RT Capitalmind: Your ESOP letter says one number. Your bank account will disagree. Here's the bit nobody explains at the townhall: when you exercise, ...
items.description_html:
RT Capitalmind
Your ESOP letter says one number. Your bank account will disagree.

Here's the bit nobody explains at the townhall: when you exercise, the gap between FMV and strike price is taxed as salary. Not when you sell. When you exercise. In cash. At your slab rate.

At ₹2 crore total income, that's roughly 39%. On a ₹2.1 crore perquisite, about ₹82 lakh leaves before you've sold a single share.

Add ₹10 lakh in strike price to exercise, and you're out ₹92 lakh while the stock is still in lock-in.

When you finally sell — assuming the price held — capital gains are calculated against FMV at exercise, not your strike. Often a small loss. Useful for set-off, not much else.

Net new wealth on a ₹1.9 crore ESOP? Closer to ₹98 lakh.

If you're sitting on ESOPs and a liquidity event is coming, read this first: https://www.capitalmind.in/insights/the-math-behind-your-esop-windfall

items.title: RT Deedy: The vibes in SF feel pretty frenetic right now. The divide in outcomes is the worst I've ever seen. Over the last 5yrs, a group of ~10k peop...
items.description_html:
RT Deedy
The vibes in SF feel pretty frenetic right now. The divide in outcomes is the worst I've ever seen.

Over the last 5yrs, a group of ~10k people - employees at Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, Nvidia, Meta TBD, founders - have hit retirement wealth of well above $20M (back of the envelope AI estimation).

Everyone outside that group feels like they can work their well-paying (but <$500k) job for their whole life and never get there.

Worse yet, layoffs are in full swing. Many software engineers feel like their life's skill is no longer useful. The day to day role of most jobs has changed overnight with AI.

As a result,
1. The corporate ladder looks like the wrong building to climb.
Everyone's trying to align with a new set of career "paths": should I be a founder? Is it too late to join Anthropic / OpenAI? should I get into AI? what company stock will 10x next? People are demanding higher salaries and switching jobs more and more.

2. There’s a deep malaise about work (and its future).
Why even work at all for “peanuts”? Will my job even exist in a few years? Many feel helpless. You hear the “permanent underclass” conversation a lot, esp from young people. It's hard to focus on doing good work when you think "man, if I joined Anthropic 2yrs ago, I could retire"

3. The mid to late middle managers feel paralyzed.
Many have families and don't feel like they have the energy or network to just "start a company". They don't particularly have any AI skills. They see the writing on the wall: middle management is being hollowed out in many companies.

4. The rich aren’t particularly happy either.
No one is shedding tears for them (and rightfully so). But those who have "made it" experience a profound lack of purpose too. Some have gone from <$150k to >$50M in a few years with no ramp. It flips your life plans upside down. For some, comparison is the thief of joy. For some, they escape to NYC to "live life". For others still, they start companies "just cuz", often to win status points. They never imagined that by age 30, they'd be set. I once asked a post-economic founder friend why they didn't just sell the co and they said "and do what? right now, everyone wants to talk to me. if i sell, I will only have money."

I understand that many reading this scoff at the champagne problems of the valley. Society is warped in this tech bubble. What is often well-off anywhere else in the world is bang average here.

Unlike many other places, tenure, intelligence and hard work can be loosely correlated with outcomes in the Bay. Living through a societally transformative gold rush in that environment can be paralyzing. "Am I in the right place? Should I move? Is there time still left? Am I gonna make it?" It psychologically torments many who have moved here in search of "success".

Ironically, a frequent side effect of this torment is to spin up the very products making everyone rich in hopes that you too can vibecode your path to economic enlightenment.

items.title: Agentic routing is such an interesting study. Built a basic agent that does planning on Opus 4 but reasons on 4.6 through a tool call (skill call). Du...
items.description_html: Agentic routing is such an interesting study. Built a basic agent that does planning on Opus 4 but reasons on 4.6 through a tool call (skill call). Due to lack of document grounding, I embedded corpus into system prompt. Worked well. Second stage, enabled managed RAG for grounding & agent stated skipping tool call entirely, no skill calls. It treated grounded data as a higher evidence deterministic choice & skipped skill call. Tweaked tool description, still no skill call. Changed skill description matching to tool call & bingo!! Agent skipped grounding, entered planning mode & made a tool call. Evals for Agentic behavior is literally out paces everything in this field. What's your such key learning from a recent build?

items.title: RT New York Magazine: Joe Lim estimates that 90 percent of what you see on the internet is advertising in disguise, and he should know. For three year...
items.description_html:
RT New York Magazine
Joe Lim estimates that 90 percent of what you see on the internet is advertising in disguise, and he should know. For three years, Lim ran a company called Floodify, which at its peak operated 65,000 dummy social-media accounts used to drum up attention on behalf of paying clients.

The point of this kind of marketing is that nobody is supposed to notice it. But lately, the machinery has started to show.

In April, Justin Bieber headlined two consecutive weekends at Coachella. Coachella is the biggest stage in pop music save only for the Super Bowl, the kind of event that in theory generates its own attention. And yet on both weekends, a Discord server writer Lane Brown had been monitoring hosted paid campaigns for Bieber’s Coachella performances, offering clippers — people who are hired to turn a song, trailer, interview, stump speech, or whatever into short, social-media-friendly fragments — as much as a dollar per thousand views.

“On social media, popular opinion is being formed, measured, and manipulated all at once, and every signal the platforms produce — a trending song, a backlash, a talking point, the feeling that ‘everybody’ is suddenly talking about the same thing — can now be fabricated by unseen actors with hidden agendas,” writes Brown.

“Everybody is doing this now,” Lim says. “And if you’re not, you’re behind.”

Brown reports on how the same techniques are now being used to fool people on every app they go to in order to find out what other people think, not just in music but across entertainment, politics, consumer products, and celebrity gossip: https://nymag.visitlink.me/w6Bu9N

items.title: RT Lalit Rathi - LKR: Easy to mock mutual fund distributors time and again. Much harder to acknowledge the role they have played in financially educat...
items.description_html:
RT Lalit Rathi - LKR
Easy to mock mutual fund distributors time and again. Much harder to acknowledge the role they have played in financially educating and bringing crores of first time investors into formal savings and capital markets.

India did not build a Rs 70 lakh crore mutual fund industry only through apps and fancy “global allocation” podcasts.

It was built ground up by thousands of MFDs who travelled city to city town to town convincing families to move from gold lockers, FD obsession and LIC-only mindset towards disciplined investing and SIP culture.

The irony is amazing.

The same intellectual ecosystem that constantly lectures India about “financialisation of savings” also uses MFDs as a punching bag every second week… especially many of those whose entire personality now revolves around selling international funds or investing in every fourth loss making glamour stock listed as 4 AM!

Apparently a guy convincing a middle class family to start a Rs 5000 SIP is the problem… not blind global momentum chasing packaged as sophisticated investing..

MFDs manage behaviour more than money.

They stop panic during crashes.
They create discipline during volatility.
They bring first generation investors into markets.
They build last mile trust in a country where financial literacy is still evolving.

Without that layer India would still be largely sitting in idle savings and insurance policies sold as investments.

Of course there are bad actors in every industry. But reducing the entire distribution ecosystem into a sarcastic one liner has now become fashionable.

Easy to joke about distributors. Far harder to build investor behaviour for 15 years through two bear markets and multiple crashes.


Neil Borate: 😅 A gem by @1shankarsharma.

items.title: Access to safe, usable toilet facilities is a critical component of public health and safety. India has long struggled with very low levels of sanitat...
items.description_html:
Access to safe, usable toilet facilities is a critical component of public health and safety.

India has long struggled with very low levels of sanitation, significantly worse than many comparable countries. In 2000, only 14% of India’s population had access to at least a basic sanitation facility, placing it behind much poorer countries in Sub-Saharan Africa, such as Somalia. When people do not have access to a safe and usable toilet, they are forced to defecate in the open.

However, over the next two decades, India made consistent progress in improving access to sanitation. By 2022, India had surpassed countries that were at a similar level in 2000, or were ahead at the time.

Basic sanitation facilities are defined as improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households. These include flush/pour-flush to piped sewer systems, septic tanks or pit latrines; ventilated improved pit latrines, composting toilets or pit latrines with slabs. This categorisation is based on WHO and UNICEF's definition.

What kind of toilet facilities do Indian households have access to? Read @nileena_suresh's piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/sanitation/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Toilets #Sanitation #WASH #India #DataForIndia

items.title: RT Nithin Kamath: One thing that feels under-discussed in all the conversations about attracting foreign capital into India is the Indian diaspora. Th...
items.description_html:
RT Nithin Kamath
One thing that feels under-discussed in all the conversations about attracting foreign capital into India is the Indian diaspora.

There is a large population of NRIs who are emotionally and financially interested in investing in India. But today, for many of them, the process of opening accounts, completing documentation, and actually investing in Indian markets is still far more painful than it needs to be.

Making life easier for NRIs could be one of the lowest-hanging fruits for attracting long-term capital into India.

This is something we’ve been focusing on heavily at @zerodha as well. Over the last year or so, we’ve made several changes to make investing as seamless as possible for NRIs. But there are still many frictions that exist because of regulatory and compliance requirements.

Hopefully, SEBI and the government look at this more closely and think about how to make it easier for NRIs to bring money into India and participate in Indian markets. For a country trying to attract global capital, the Indian diaspora seems like the most obvious place to start.

items.description_html:
RT Markets by Zerodha
Re By 2029, Ember estimates the short-term warming impact of India's coal mine methane could reach about 138 million tonnes of CO2-equivalent. That's roughly equal to the CO2 emissions from every truck and bus on Indian roads in 2021. Underground mining is a bigger culprit, making up 15% of methane emissions.

items.title: RT Imtiaz Mahmood: I was on a train in Tokyo. We stopped between stations. Announcement in Japanese, then in English: "We apologize for the delay. We ...
items.description_html:
RT Imtiaz Mahmood
I was on a train in Tokyo. We stopped between stations. Announcement in Japanese, then in English: "We apologize for the delay. We will resume shortly."

The delay was maybe 3 minutes. Not a big deal.

When the train started moving again, another announcement: "We sincerely apologize for the delay. We were stopped for 3 minutes and 20 seconds. This is unacceptable. Thank you for your patience."

Three minutes and twenty seconds. They measured it exactly. And called it unacceptable.

When I got off at my stop, there were station staff on the platform bowing and handing out delay certificates.

I took one out of curiosity. It was an official document stating that the train had been delayed by 3 minutes and 20 seconds, signed and stamped.

The staff member said in English "for your employer. So they know the delay was not your fault."

I said I'm a tourist, I don't need it. He looked confused. "But the delay affected you. You deserve an apology."

Three minutes. They were treating a three-minute delay like a major incident.

Later I mentioned this to a Japanese friend. They said "oh yes, delay certificates are normal. Trains are supposed to be exactly on time. If they are late, they must apologize."

I said three minutes isn't late, it's nothing. My friend said "in Japan, three minutes is late. On time means on time. Not approximately on time."

They said the train company probably investigated why there was a 3-minute delay. "They will find the cause and fix it so it doesn't happen again."

I kept the certificate. It's framed in my apartment now. A reminder that somewhere in the world, people care about three minutes.

© 6IX.

@BSAT_Properties

items.title: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solut...
items.description_html: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solutions (Claude, OpenAi). However, this defense collapses the moment we have a precedence from a big enterprise opening up just the way enterprises opened up to cloud after a decade long friction. The moment this starts happening, the JVs OpenAi & Claude have formed will make a killing. And, what will take this wall down? For e.g., enterprises are currently very upset after SAP API policy that prohibited clients from using their own data & sending it to external tools via APIs to architect a solution outside of SAP. This can be the 1st hammer in the wall. One wrong step & clients will find ways to disconnect with enterprise OEMs & will be willing to trust(with an agreement perhaps) likes of Claude & OpenAi. The moment this happens, it is done!! This is the biggest risk I foresee.

items.title: RT Rajeev Mantri: 1/ Owing to high barriers to entry, India’s lodging and hotels industry has become a cartel that raises prices at will and fleeces ...
items.description_html:
RT Rajeev Mantri
1/ Owing to high barriers to entry, India’s lodging and hotels industry has become a cartel that raises prices at will and fleeces customers.

India’s luxury hotel average daily rate (ADR) in Mumbai and Delhi now rival those of New York and London. Absurdly enough, the financial press sometimes celebrates this as some triumph of Indian hospitality. It is nothing of the sort. High prices are the unmistakable stamp of a supply-constrained market in a low income country, which should actually have a very competitively priced and deep pool of lodging options. The hotel industry is a structural failure being hyped as a success.

items.title: Higher education is seen as a key to getting young Indians into skilled, high-paying jobs and accelerating economic growth. Just under 40 million youn...
items.description_html:
Higher education is seen as a key to getting young Indians into skilled, high-paying jobs and accelerating economic growth.

Just under 40 million young people (aged 18-23) were enrolled in higher education programmes in India as of 2022. This included both undergraduate (or equivalent) and postgraduate (or equivalent) levels of study.

However, at 32%, enrolment in tertiary or higher education in India is still below the world average.

In the early 1990s, India’s higher education enrolment rates were similar to China’s, for instance. But in the two decades since, China has made big strides: more than seven in ten young Chinese adults are now in higher education, compared to three in ten young Indian adults.

How does higher education enrolment look across states, and what disciplines do students choose to study in India? Read @akwaghmare’s piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/higher-education/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#College #Undergraduate #Education #HigherEducation #India #DataForIndia

items.title: RT Anthony DiGiorgio, DO, MHA: This is the ultimate midwit healthcare take. No, 32 countries have not “figured out” universal healthcare. The UK has...
items.description_html:
RT Anthony DiGiorgio, DO, MHA
This is the ultimate midwit healthcare take.

No, 32 countries have not “figured out” universal healthcare.

The UK has “free” healthcare, and roughly 1 in 3 cancer patients in England still fail to start treatment within 62 days of urgent referral.

Canada has “free” healthcare, and the median wait for neurosurgical treatment is around a year.

Australia has “free” healthcare, and over half the country still buys private insurance despite paying for a public universal system with their taxes.

Switzerland has universal coverage, because residents are required to buy private insurance. There is no government system where benevolent bureaucrats tuck you in at night with a warm blanket and an MRI appointment.

The actual lesson from other wealthy countries is not “they figured it out.”

America’s system has huge problems. Our prices are insane, insurance markets are distorted, and hospital systems are cartelized. Our regulations make care more expensive than it needs to be.

Yet we still guarantee access to even the 8% who don’t have coverage. We give easy routes to qualify for medicaid for those with disabilities.

Pretending the rest of the world solved healthcare because they slapped the word “universal” on a rationing scheme is not analysis.

It is bumper sticker policy for people who think access means having a card in your wallet while you wait a year to see the doctor you need.


daz: Free Universal Healthcare is so complicated and expensive that only 32 of the 33 wealthiest countries in the world have figured it out.

items.title: RT Pritesh Gupta: most debates fail before they even begin because the problem is framed wrong from the start. people think they are arguing about sol...
items.description_html:
RT Pritesh Gupta
most debates fail before they even begin because the problem is framed wrong from the start.

people think they are arguing about solutions, but often they are only reacting to symptoms. the real issue stays hidden underneath. a good framework helps you move from what is visible to what is actually causing it.

this is why many indian systems remain stuck. the focus stays on inputs: money spent, schemes launched, announcements made. but inputs are not outcomes. distributing tablets is not the same as children learning. activity gets measured because it is easy to count. outcomes get ignored because they are harder to see.

whoever defines the problem usually controls the solution. frame a learning crisis as an infrastructure issue and the answer becomes buildings and devices. frame it as an information issue and the answer becomes better data, feedback, and accountability. same reality, different response.

that is why framing is not just intellectual, it is political. the way a problem is named decides who gets blamed, what progress looks like, and whose reality matters. which is why getting the question right is often harder, and far more important, than finding an answer.

check out: http://frameworks.pranaykotas.com by @pranaykotas

If you're confused about how to navigate the market, what stocks to buy, what to sell, it is because you dont have a system or an anchor which can guide your decisions.

Newsflow driven investing can drive us crazy and is extremely unprofitable.

A system gives you coherence in thoughts and the clarity to execute. It need not be rigid and its not meant to be machine like. It should more like an anchor around which you can think clearly and decide.

For me the anchors are earnings and growth that drive my actions.

Start systemizing your investing. It's one of the biggest edges out there.

items.title: RT nikhilv: There are a few reasons for this - Inequality literature in Claude's pretraining data is 'actionable', the model sees this as a problem to...
items.description_html:
RT nikhilv
There are a few reasons for this -
Inequality literature in Claude's pretraining data is 'actionable', the model sees this as a problem to be solved. Conversely, literature around growth is framed descriptively, like matter-of-fact. So the model has learnt that anyone serious about public policy will be more concerned about inequality than growth.
Anthropic's constitution is also to blame - it introduces priors, some explicitly mentioned (avoid harms to vulnerable groups/be fair etc - inequality concerns follow naturally). And then you have RLHF from US/UK left of centre cohorts, and black box reveals itself.
Try grok and you'll see a mirror image - xAI tuned the priors the other way, so it may flip rather than disappear @pranaykotas @srajagopalan @amitvarma @AshishPonders @acorn

items.title: The rate of murder, or ‘intentional homicide’, is widely used as an indicator of public safety in a city or country. India recorded nearly 37,500 in...
items.description_html:
The rate of murder, or ‘intentional homicide’, is widely used as an indicator of public safety in a city or country.

India recorded nearly 37,500 incidents of intentional homicides in 2023.

The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) considers statistics put out by India's National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) under three offences to calculate intentional homicides in India.

First, culpable homicide, that is murder (punishable under Sec. 103 under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita or previously, Section 302 of the Indian Penal Code). Four in five intentional homicides in India are murders.

Second, dowry deaths (defined and punishable under Section 80 of BNS or previously, Section 304B of IPC), accounting for nearly one in six intentional homicides.

The remaining are culpable homicides not amounting to murder (punishable under Sec. 105 under BNS or previously, Section 304 of IPC), an offence that is considered less serious than murder. One example of this would be a sudden fight between two people where one of them kills the other only in the heat of the moment, without any premeditation to cause the death.

How has the incidence of intentional homicide changed over time in India? Read Ameya Bokil’s piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/homicide/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Murder #Homicide #Law #India #DataForIndia
